content:
1. INTRODUCTION:  
 
This document contains the resources required for organizing and maintaining the student’s 
clubs & chapters at the school of CS&AI. This compilation of materials and practices includes 
advice about recruiting members, ideas for activities, how to schedule for running 
meetings and achieve milestones and much more. Objective: To enable students’ participation in the development of the University. Their 
career, personality, and organizational skills through interactive programs. Composition: The Dean of the school of CS&AI will be the Chief Patron and Faculty 
coordinator/mentor be the Patron of the Students Club. Each club will have an Executive 
Committee and Representatives. Executive Committee  
School of CS&AI student clubs are required to have a Chair, Vice Chair, Secretary, 
Treasurer, Marketing & PR secretary and other possible operational roles who are the 
SoCS&AI students, as well as a Faculty Sponsor. The major criteria of Executive Committee 
will be as follows:  
Executive Committee 
1. Chairperson: 2nd & 3rd Year Students  
2. Vice-Chair: From 1st to 3rd year Students 
3. Secretary: From 1st to 3rd year Students  
4. Treasurer: From 1st to 3rd year Students 
5. Marketing & PR Secretary: From 1st to 3rd year Students 
Operational Committee 
6. Web Master: From 1st to 3rd year Students 
7. Membership Chair: From 1st to 3rd year Students 
8. Management Head: From 1st to 3rd year Students 
9. Content & Creative Head: From 1st to 3rd year Students 
10. Digital & Social Media Head: From 1st to 3rd year Students 
Please Note that two more additional Positions can be created by the individual Club/Chapter 
as per the need but no more than 12 members are allowed in the Core Committee (Executive

Team Level 1 and Operational Team Level 2). On record it must be submitted to the Office 
once the Committee/Club is formed. 11. Faculty Mentor must be from the School of Computer Science & AI 
 
2. RESPONSIBILITIES OF CLUBS: 
 
Club office bearers are charged with meeting the needs of their members, activities and other 
responsibilities given by Dean. Clubs are responsible for maintaining the frequency and quality 
of activity in the area they serve, and for identifying and promoting those activities of specific 
interest to their members. Each club must communicate with the faculty mentor; submit annual 
financial and activity reports; report changes in officers and contact information; update club 
member lists, and management of funds. This section defined the duties and responsibilities of each club in addition to common 
responsibilities. 2.1 Duties and Key Responsibilities of The Core Committee of each club/chapter: 
 
1. Coding Club: 
Faculty Coordinator: Dr. P. Pramod Kumar 
 
1 
Target activities to improve problem solving skills of school students 
2 
Motivate students to improve rank on at any Level of Coding Platform (Beginner, 
Intermediate & Advanced) 
3 
Motivate students to participate competitions organize by Coding Club in-campus and 
off-campus both. 4 
Explore other benefits of Multiple Coding Platforms 
5 
Interaction & Orientation with 1st  Year students for membership  
 
2. IEEE Computer Society: 
Faculty Coordinator: Dr. Mohammad Ali  
 
(Please note that of this Society all the core members must have the active IEEE Student 
Membership Minimum 12 Membership are required to run it) 
 
1 
Explore support IEEE provide and connect international expert from IEEE platform 
2 
Focus on topics such as product technology, protocols, and standards. 3 
IEEE Day Celebration, Vool 
4 
Increase IEEE membership among students and faculties. 5 
Technical Development:: Technical Student Paper Contests, Write articles for 
Potentials magazine, Robotic/ Design Contests, IEEEXtreme programming challenge 
6 
Professional Development Professional Activities Workshops, Student Professional 
Awareness Conference (S-PAC), Student Professional Awareness Venture (S-PAVe). 7 
Humanitarian Development Special projects, community awareness activities, get 
involved with Student Branch activities on campus.

3. Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) Chapter:  
Faculty coordinator: Dr. Raghvendra Kishore Singh 
 
(Please note that for this Chapter, all the core members must have the active ACM Student 
Membership Minimum 12-15 Membership are required to run it) 
 
 
1 
ACM student club is suggested to maintain visibility on national and international 
landscape and maintain high standard in organizing events. 2 
ACM student club focus should be towards research topics. 3 
ACM student club need to increase their reachability by contacting expert for talk from 
ACM portal. 4 
Administrative Interface —manage your club’s member roster, update your club’s 
contact information, and much more 
5 
Manage ACM Club Web Tools 
6 
Local Activities Calendar 
7 
Take advantage of ACM Distinguished Speakers Program 
8 
UPE/ACM Student Club Scholarship Award 
9 
ACM Student Club Excellence Awards 
 
 
 
4. Computer Society of India (CSI) Chapter  
Faculty coordinator: Ms. Neelima Gurrapu  
 
Please note that for this Chapter, all the core members must have the active CSI Student 
Membership to maintain a number of membership. 1 
CSI student club is advised to boost up activity. 2 
CSI student club is suggested to focus on organizing specialization specific training 
and workshops. 3 
CSI club should consider technology development and needs for India perspective. 4 
CSI club should consider events for students’ development on social skills. 5 
Interaction & Orientation with 1st  Year students for membership 
 
 
 
5. Sankhya Club 
Faculty coordinator: Dr. Sandeep (Mathematics) 
 
1 
Organize workshops and seminars: Invite renowned mathematicians to conduct 
workshops and seminars on various mathematical topics. 2 
Assist students in preparing for exams like GRE, GMAT, or GATE. 3 
Advocate for the needs and interests of mathematics students at the university level. 4 
Motivate students to engage in research projects related to mathematics.

9. Hackathon Student Club: 
Faculty coordinator: Mr. Durgunala Ranjith 
 
1 
Measure the quantity and quality of hackathons organized by the club, including participation 
rates, feedback, and impact. 2 
Track the number of students involved in club activities, their level of engagement, and 
satisfaction with the club's events. 3 
Assess the growth of students' technical skills, problem-solving abilities, and teamwork 
through pre- and post-hackathon assessments. 4 
Measure the number and quality of partnerships established with industry mentors, sponsors, 
and organizations. 5 
Track the success of students in hackathons, including awards, recognition, and career 
opportunities. 6 
Support and help to other Clubs and Students for conducting Hackathon and Participating in 
it. 10. Digital Design Media & Branding Club: 
Faculty coordinator: Mr. S. Deepan 
 
1 
Skill Development: Organize workshops, seminars, and guest lectures on digital media, 
branding, and marketing. Provide hands-on experience and mentorship opportunities for 
students. 2 
Project Success: Encourage students to work on individual or group projects that apply digital 
media and branding concepts. Provide feedback, mentorship, and resources to ensure project 
success. 3 
Industry Collaborations: Reach out to industry professionals and organizations for guest 
lectures, workshops, and internship opportunities. Facilitate networking events and 
collaborations. 4 
Student Engagement: Organize regular club meetings, events, and competitions to foster a 
sense of community and engagement. Promote active participation and leadership 
opportunities. 5 
Career Outcomes: Track student placements, internships, and entrepreneurial ventures related 
to digital media and branding. Provide career counselling and guidance to help students 
achieve their goals. 11. Cloud Computing & DevOps Student Club  
Faculty coordinator: Mr. Sharan Bediga 
 
1 
Organize workshops and training sessions on cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP), DevOps 
tools (Git, Jenkins, Docker), and relevant programming languages (Python, Java). 2 
Collaborate with industry professionals for guest lectures, mentorship programs, and 
internship opportunities. 3 
Host hackathons and coding competitions to challenge students and showcase their skills.

3. RESPONSIBILITIES OF CLUB OFFICERS  
 
Each club will have a core committee, which includes elected club officers, the past 
Chairperson. The club’s core committee is generally responsible for managing the club’s affairs 
(except for electing officers and amending bylaws). It is essential for the core committee to be open to the suggestions and feedback of the club 
membership and should always be open to offers of help. Upon assuming the office, all core committee members should contact the faculty mentor to 
introduce him/herself, review club bylaws and policies and procedures, and review the events 
of the past year with his/her predecessors. The Club Chair  
 
The Chair is the principal officer and is responsible for leading the Club and managing its activities in 
accordance with the policies and procedures of School. The club Chairperson has the overall responsibility for developing club programs, for ensuring 
that plans and assignments are carried out, and for seeing that the club fulfils its obligations to 
the Association. The Chairperson should be familiar with the policies and procedures of the club, with the club’s 
bylaws, and with the duties of all the other club officers and committee members. The newly elected Chairperson should schedule an executive committee meeting which 
includes the outgoing officers as well. At that time, the Chairperson required to schedule 
executive council meetings for the entire year. Keep in mind the advantages of scheduling the 
meeting at the same time each month. Before getting too far along in the club’s year, the 
Chairperson should do the following: appoint or establish nominating committee according to 
bylaws for next year’s elections; communicate activities of the club to the faculty mentor; set 
audit procedures in motion at year end for financial records turnover; and plan a budget with 
the club Treasurer, to be reviewed and approved by the entire core committee. One of the most important duties of the club Chairperson is finding enthusiastic and qualified 
members to serve as executive committee members. The Chairperson should strive to bring 
new students from first year into the committee structure to provide training for future club 
leaders. It is strongly recommended that the club chairperson take the time at general meetings to 
acknowledge those club volunteers whose help has been valuable. This positive feedback and 
public recognition of good works is often just the encouragement an active volunteer needs to 
commit to a longer-term leadership position in the club in future years.

The Club Vice Chair  
The club Vice Chairperson should be prepared to assume the office of Chairperson or 
temporarily act in that position at any time, assist the Chair in the management of the Club, and 
perform other duties that may be assigned by the Chair. The Vice Chairperson should be 
familiar with the duties and responsibilities of the Chairperson, and of the other elected officers 
and committee chairpersons. The Vice Chairperson can be an integral component of the club’s operation. In most cases, 
the Vice Chairperson is responsible for overseeing the activities and progress of the various 
committee and club programs. The Club Treasurer  
The club’s financial management and operation are of great importance to the club. The club 
Treasurer is responsible for the fiscal operation and reporting of the club, and of all committees 
and programs established by the club. The ongoing responsibilities of the club treasurer include:  
1. Maintaining all club financial records. 2. Filing mandatory annual financial report with School. 3. Reporting financial status and requirements to club. 4. Contacting sponsors for financial aid in different event organized by the club. The Club Secretary  
The ongoing responsibilities of the Club Secretary include:   
1. Informing faculty mentor of the names and contact information of new officers and providing 
School with updated membership lists. 2. Sending agenda to committee members for executive meetings and recording the minutes of 
such meetings. 3. Handling all club correspondence on a timely basis. 4. Maintaining club files for historical purposes. The Club Marketing & PR Secretary 
The role of a Student Club Marketing & PR Secretary typically involves managing the club's 
public relations, marketing strategies, and communication efforts. This person is responsible 
for promoting the club's activities, events, and brand, ensuring that the club maintains a positive 
image both within the school and in the wider community. Specific duties might include: 
Social Media Management: Overseeing the club's social media accounts, posting updates, and 
engaging with followers.

Event Promotion: Creating and distributing promotional materials for events, such as posters, 
flyers, and online content. Communication: Drafting and sending newsletters or emails to club members and other 
stakeholders. Brand Management: Ensuring that all promotional materials are consistent with the club’s 
branding guidelines. Public Relations: Managing the club’s relationships with other organizations, sponsors, and the 
media. The Faculty Mentor  
The Mentor shall be a faculty member of school. Each Student Club has one Mentor. The 
faculty mentor shall be generally responsible for the activities of the Club. Specifically,  
1. The Mentor helps provide continuity from year to year as student leadership and 
personnel change. 2. Promotes good student-faculty relationships. 3. Helps maintain University/School standards in all activities of the Club. 4. Exercises financial supervision, if necessary, by promoting prompt payment of bills and 
collection of dues and overseeing the settlement of all accounts in the event of 
dissolution of the Club. 4. IDEAS FOR CLUB ACTIVITIES  
 
The kinds of activities a club can undertake are endless, depending on the creativity and interest 
of each group. But here are a few examples of activities that may be of interest to particular 
club:  
Distinguished Speakers Program/Lectures: Invite one of the Distinguished Speakers to speak with 
your club on a topic of your choice. Plan the event with other local organizations and publicize it well 
to recruit a big audience or even new club members. Panel Discussions and Roundtables: Invite three or four people from the technology workforce or 
organize the event with members from your club. This will encourage discussions and exchange of 
ideas and opinions. Joint Meetings: Joint meetings are a great way to interact with local professionals. They are also an 
opportunity for Professional Clubs to meet with local Student Clubs to teach and advise. Mixers/Social Events: Host an event, dinner, or dessert party where the main purpose is to meet and 
greet. Encourage others to interact and exchange thoughts and ideas. For a change of pace, these events 
can be hosted at bowling alleys, bars, restaurants or at a park on a nice day. Sports Teams: Enlist your club in a local sporting league. This will promote unity and bonding amongst 
club members, as well as encourage friendly competition! Holiday Parties: Celebrate the holidays together with food, drinks, games and prizes! A great chance 
to get to know all the members and officers better.

Interdisciplinary Activities: Invite students who are double-majoring or otherwise pursuing 
interdisciplinary paths, along with faculty members who perform interdisciplinary research to speak 
about projects. Examples include digital art, artificial life, mathematical simulation, etc. Profile current 
tutors, during a club meeting, encouraging the students to discuss the benefits/advantages of their work. Book Club: Invite students to participate in a monthly book club meeting, where they are free to discuss 
any interesting novels, but are encouraged to read computer science related books. Oral Histories: People have valuable stories to share with succeeding “generations” of students. Have 
people tape each other and edit the footage into stories. Ask each pair to describe how each member 
chose computing as a major, what struggles s/he has had and how s/he overcame adversity, and her 
future plans.

If you require to provide a certificate after hosting an event. In order to issue a certificate, we need the 
following information:  
1. Date of Event  
2. Name of Event  
3. Name of club who will be the certificate holder  
4. Location of event, including address  
5. Type of event  
6. Summary of the event 
6. Name and contact details, affiliation of participants  
7. Number of volunteers  
8. Contact info to email certificate (name, fax, email)  
Please submit this information to respective Club Faculty Mentor and Office at most one week of 
the event. Club Outreach and Communication 
Social media is a great way to stay in touch with club members and potential members. All clubs are 
encouraged to create and maintain LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook pages and Twitter accounts for their 
clubs. Create informative and interactive posts, announce upcoming meetings, connect with other clubs 
in the area and show the community what makes your club special. Financial Responsibilities and Requirements 
The following is a summary of financial responsibilities:  
1. Any club that collects, holds or disburses funds must submit an annual accounting of such funds. 2. Disbursements of funds for expenditures necessary for the normal operation of the club require 
approval from faculty mentor. Any club desiring to disburse funds beyond those necessary for normal 
operations must obtain advance approval of the Dean, SOCS&AI. 6. RECRUITMENT PROCESS  
Benefits of participating: 
Students should consider this an extracurricular activity because it helps to build character, 
leadership, decision making, organization skills, and responsibility. Procedure 
All the student office bearers are the nominated posts. This nomination is purely based on the 
merit of their leadership activities exhibited during the previous years. The Chairperson will 
be nominated by the Dean in consultation with the faculty coordinator. Other members of the 
core committee are nominated by Chairperson of the club respectively. Appropriate gender 
balance in each club shall be given priority.

• Establish productive working relationships with alumni. Communication & Interpersonal Skills 
• Have the superb interpersonal skills needed to serve as the public face of the club and 
to form genuine connections with constituents at all levels. • Personify energy, a positive attitude, emotional strength, empathy, integrity, and 
enthusiasm for service. • Passionately communicate a vision of research, education, and service to inspire and 
motivate others. • Enthusiastically welcome opportunities to participate in the everyday social and 
cultural life of the school, University and surrounding community. Point distribution of selection criteria:  
 
Criteria 
Weightage 
Leadership skills: School level accomplishments as team 
leader 
10 
Executive Skills: Previous Club membership/volunteer Activity or 
accomplishment that justify your nomination 
10 
Communication Skills: Social media participation to show 
your activeness and follower strength. 10 
Interpersonal Skills: Recommendation from 2 person in 
support of your position for chairperson. 10 
Advocacy Skills: Video submission mentioning why you 
should be select as a chairperson of a club  
10 
Interview with Dean and faculty mentor 
50 
Total 
100 
 
Nomination Form is given below for School club/chapters  
 
https://forms.gle/JYsPxSv1qqUWxi9f7 
 
7. CLUB BYLAWS: 
The Club is organized and will be operated exclusively for educational and scientific purposes 
to promote the following: An increased knowledge of and greater interest in the science, design, 
development, construction, languages, management and applications of modern computing b 
Greater interest in computing and its applications c A means of communication between 
persons having an interest in computing 2. The Club will serve students at and other interested 
persons in the community. • Membership in the Student Club shall be open to all SOCS&AI students. • Membership shall not be restricted according to race, creed, age, gender, disability, 
nationality, sexual orientation, economic status, veteran status, or parenthood.

• The officers of this Club shall be: Chair, Vice Chair, secretary, Treasurer and marketing 
secretary. • Appointment or nomination of an individual to an officer position within a Club shall 
be in general limited to one year term in the same position. • An annual election meeting should be held. At this meeting, the Treasurer will present 
the required reports. Also, the election of officers shall be held. • Notices of all meetings shall be distributed to all members at least one week prior to 
any meeting. • All proposed changes to these Club Bylaws shall be approved by Dean, SOCS&AI. • Attendance- Not attending 3 or more meetings could result in a dismal from the club. • Conduct- Students who are issued code of conduct warning throughout the year could 
result in a dismal from the student council. • Attitude- Students should always represent University in the best way possible. Students should have a good/helpful attitude during all club events, and not doing so 
could result in dismissal. • No candidate shall create mutual hatred or cause tension between different castes and 
communities, religious or linguistic or between any group(s) of students. • Candidates shall refrain from criticism of all aspects of private life not connected with 
the public activities of other candidates. • All candidates shall be prohibited from indulging or abetting all activities which are 
considered to be corrupt practices and offences. Thanks, and regards, 
 
Dean Office 
School of CS&AI 
SR University

Learning and Development 
Opportunities Conducted by School
MAY
SR UNIVERSITY
Fostering Innovation: Poster 
Presentation at SR University
The School of CS & AI, SR University, in collaboration with the 
ACM Club and ISAN Club, organized a Poster Presentation for 
B.Tech students, concluding the ACM Club’s academic year 
activities. The event saw over 110 participants showcasing 
innovative ideas and analytical skills. Led by Mr. Hrudai, Mr. Divyanshu Shekhar, and Mr. Sateesh Chandra, with guidance 
from Dr. Raghwendra Kishore Singh, Dr. Santosh Kumar 
Henge, and support from Dr. Indrajeet Gupta (Dean, CS & AI), 
the event fostered creativity and collaborative learning. The Data Science Club of CS & AI, SR University successfully 
organized a Hackathon on Kaggle, challenging students to 
enhance their data science skills through hands-on problem-
solving and analytics. Participants showcased innovative 
approaches and were recognized for their exceptional 
performance. The event was led by Alleti Pranay (Chair), Vinay 
Kokkisa (Vice-Chair), and coordinated by Sandeep Kumar 
(Faculty Coordinator), inspiring students to pursue excellence 
in data-driven technologies.

SR University Technical Clubs & Student Chapters 
Club Metadata: Cybersecurity Club 
Institution: SR University 
Category: Campus Club / Technical Society 
Domain: Cybersecurity / Information Security 
Location: Campus 
Document Version: 1.0 
Source URL: https://www.sruclub.in/cybersecurity-club 
Last Updated: 25 February 2026 
 
Cybersecurity Club 
Overview 
The Cybersecurity Club at SR University serves as a dedicated platform for students interested in information security, 
ethical hacking, and cyber defense. The club focuses on building practical skills in cybersecurity through hands-on training, 
workshops, and competitive events. The Cybersecurity Club collaborates with industry partners such as Wydur Cyber Secure 
LLC to deliver high-impact learning experiences. Vision and Mission 
Vision: To continually educate students about cyber awareness and keep members abreast of emerging technologies in 
cyberspace. The Cybersecurity Club aims to develop into a model club recognized for excellence in cybersecurity education. Mission: 
• 
To inculcate cyber ethics and cybersecurity practices among club members 
• 
To learn the latest trends and techniques in the cyber world 
• 
To improve cyber awareness among rural communities as part of the Digital India initiative 
• 
To prepare young minds for careers in the cybersecurity profession 
Objectives 
The Cybersecurity Club pursues the following objectives: 
• 
Conduct technical training programmes on security tools and methodologies 
• 
Help students master security tools through hands-on sessions and competitions 
• 
Educate the student community on capabilities to prevent and respond to cyber threats 
• 
Help students implement security concepts in academic projects 
• 
Conduct cyber awareness programmes for rural populations 
Activities and Events 
The Cybersecurity Club organizes the following activities: 
• 
Cyber Strike Workshop: A high-impact workshop conducted in collaboration with Wydur Cyber Secure LLC, 
focusing on practical cybersecurity skills and industry practices 
• 
Ethical Hacking Workshops: Regular sessions on penetration testing, vulnerability assessment, and security 
auditing 
• 
Capture The Flag (CTF) Competitions: Internal and inter-college security challenges to test practical skills 
• 
Bug Bounty Training: Sessions on identifying and reporting security vulnerabilities

• 
Cyber Awareness Campaigns: Outreach programmes for rural communities on digital safety 
• 
Burp Suite Masterclass: Expert-led workshops on web application security testing 
• 
Network Security Workshops: Training on securing network infrastructure and protocols 
Membership Details 
The Cybersecurity Club is open to all students of SR University interested in information security. Membership benefits 
include access to specialized workshops, participation in CTF competitions, mentorship from industry professionals, and 
opportunities to work on real-world security projects. Leadership and Faculty Coordinators 
Faculty Coordinator: Dr. Indrajeet Gupta, Dean, School of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence 
Student Leadership: Elected student coordinators manage day-to-day operations and event organization 
Contact Information 
Institution: SR University, Warangal, Telangana 
Department: School of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence 
Faculty Email: Available through departmental contact 
Achievements 
• 
Successful organization of Cyber Strike workshop in collaboration with Wydur Cyber Secure LLC 
• 
Conducted multiple ethical hacking workshops with industry experts 
• 
Active participation in national-level cybersecurity competitions

Hackathon Student Club 
Overview 
The Hackathon Student Club at SR University is dedicated to fostering innovation, coding excellence, and collaborative 
problem-solving through intensive hackathon events. The Hackathon Student Club organizes 12-hour and 24-hour coding 
marathons that bring together students from diverse technical backgrounds to build innovative solutions. Vision and Mission 
Vision: To establish SR University as a hub for innovation and technical excellence through regular hackathon events that 
challenge students to push their creative and technical boundaries. Mission: 
• 
To provide a platform for students to transform ideas into working prototypes 
• 
To foster collaboration between students from different technical disciplines

• 
To connect students with industry mentors and real-world problem statements 
• 
To prepare students for national and international hackathon competitions 
Objectives 
The Hackathon Student Club aims to: 
• 
Organize regular internal hackathons for skill development 
• 
Host qualifying rounds for major external hackathon competitions 
• 
Facilitate team formation and project management skills 
• 
Provide mentorship and technical guidance during hackathon events 
• 
Showcase student innovations to industry partners and investors 
Activities and Events 
The Hackathon Student Club conducts the following events: 
• 
12-Hour Hackathon: Internal coding marathon organized on 27 April 2024 for 1st and 3rd year students, focusing 
on rapid prototyping and innovation 
• 
24-Hour Hackathon: Extended innovation challenge with participation from over 100 teams, showcasing 
advanced coding skills and creative solutions 
• 
TechStorm Hackathon: Collaborative event organized with the Coding Club and Computer Society of India 
(CSI) SRU Chapter 
• 
AWS GenAI Internal Challenge: Qualifying event for "Hack the Future with AWS" GenAI Hackathon, 
challenging students to build functional Generative AI applications using AWS PartyRock 
• 
Kaggle Competitions: Data science hackathons organized in collaboration with the Data Science Club 
• 
Industry Problem Statements: Hackathons featuring real challenges from partner organizations 
Membership Details 
Membership in the Hackathon Student Club is open to all SR University students with an interest in coding and innovation. Members receive priority registration for hackathon events, access to pre-hackathon training sessions, and networking 
opportunities with industry mentors. Leadership and Faculty Coordinators 
Faculty Support: Dr. Indrajeet Gupta, Dean, School of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence 
Collaborating Entities: Coding Club SR University, Computer Society of India (CSI) SRU Chapter 
Contact Information 
Institution: SR University, Warangal, Telangana 
Department: School of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence 
Achievements 
• 
Successfully organized 24-hour hackathon with 100+ participating teams 
• 
Hosted multiple 12-hour internal hackathons with strong student participation 
• 
Collaborated with AWS for GenAI hackathon challenges 
• 
Partnership with Coding Club and CSI for TechStorm events

• 
Collaborative Projects: Multi-student projects hosted on GitHub to simulate real-world development 
environments 
• 
Industry Speaker Sessions: Talks by professionals on software development best practices 
Membership Details 
The GitHub Community welcomes all SR University students interested in software development. Membership provides 
access to workshops, mentorship for open-source contributions, and opportunities to collaborate on community projects. Leadership and Faculty Coordinators 
Faculty Coordinator: Dr. Pramod Kumar Poladi 
Dean: Dr. Indrajeet Gupta, School of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence 
Head of Department: Dr. Sheshikala Martha, Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence 
Contact Information 
Institution: SR University, Warangal, Telangana 
Department: School of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence 
Achievements 
• 
Successful collaboration with ACM-W Chapter for career development sessions 
• 
Active participation in open-source contribution programmes 
• 
Regular workshops on GitHub and version control practices

Last Updated: 25 February 2026 
 
Cloud Computing & DevOps Student Club 
Overview 
The Cloud Computing & DevOps Student Club at SR University is dedicated to empowering the next generation of cloud 
professionals. The Cloud Computing & DevOps Student Club provides practical, hands-on training in cloud technologies 
and DevOps practices. The Cloud Computing & DevOps Student Club has trained over 250 students in cloud computing 
through a practical lab-first approach using Amazon Web Services (AWS). Vision and Mission 
Vision: To serve as a launchpad for careers in cloud computing and DevOps by providing industry-relevant skills and 
certifications. Mission: 
• 
To make students learn cloud computing through practical implementation 
• 
To prepare students for industry-recognized cloud certifications 
• 
To bridge the gap between academic theory and cloud industry requirements

Institution: SR University 
Category: Campus Club / Entrepreneurship

Student Startup Innovator Club 
Overview 
The Student Startup Innovator Club at SR University nurtures entrepreneurial mindsets and supports students in 
transforming innovative ideas into viable startups. The Student Startup Innovator Club works closely with SR Innovation 
Exchange (SRiX), the Technology Business Incubator of SR University recognized by the Department of Science and 
Technology (DST). Vision and Mission 
Vision: To create a vibrant entrepreneurial ecosystem at SR University that produces successful startups and innovative 
solutions to real-world problems. Mission: 
• 
To identify and nurture startup ideas from students 
• 
To provide resources required to build products and find customer acceptance 
• 
To support startups in gearing for growth through access to global partners and investors 
• 
To bring entrepreneurs, mentors, researchers, and academicians together 
Objectives 
The Student Startup Innovator Club pursues these objectives: 
• 
Identify promising startup ideas from SR University students 
• 
Provide mentorship and resources for product development 
• 
Facilitate connections with investors and venture capitalists 
• 
Organize pitch events and demo days for student startups 
• 
Support participation in national and international startup competitions 
Activities and Events 
The Student Startup Innovator Club organizes: 
• 
Idea Validation Workshops: Sessions to help students refine and validate startup concepts 
• 
Pitch Practice Events: Opportunities to present ideas to mentors and potential investors 
• 
Startup Mentorship Programmes: Pairing students with experienced entrepreneurs and industry experts 
• 
Product Development Support: Access to resources for prototyping and building minimum viable products 
• 
Funding Guidance: Assistance with accessing seed funding and grants through SRiX 
• 
Networking Events: Connections with SRiX's network of 171+ supported startups 
Membership Details

The Student Startup Innovator Club is open to students with entrepreneurial aspirations. Members receive access to SRiX 
resources, mentorship, and funding opportunities. Leadership and Faculty Coordinators 
Institution Support: SR Innovation Exchange (SRiX) - DST Recognized Technology Business Incubator 
Key Metrics: SRiX has supported over 171 startups, created over 741 jobs, funded 103 startups with approximately Rs 
13.57 Crore towards ideation, prototyping, and seed funding 
Contact Information 
Institution: SR University, Warangal, Telangana 
Incubator: SR Innovation Exchange (SRiX) 
Website: srix.in 
Achievements 
• 
SRiX has supported over 171 startups to date 
• 
Creation of over 741 jobs through supported startups 
• 
Funded startups have raised over Rs 76 Crores from VCs and Angels 
• 
DST recognition as a Technology Business Incubator

Computer Society of India (CSI) Chapter 
Overview 
The Computer Society of India (CSI) Student Chapter at SR University is an official student branch of the Computer Society 
of India, the first and largest body of computer professionals in India. The CSI SRU Chapter was inaugurated on 16 January 
2024 and has quickly established itself as a key technical society within the university. Vision and Mission 
Vision: To align with CSI's national mission of promoting excellence in computing and information technology while 
providing SR University students with professional development opportunities. Mission: 
• 
To encourage students to become CSI nominee members 
• 
To involve students in CSI Students Webinar Series, conferences, and tutorials 
• 
To organize seminars, workshops, and competitions in association with CSI 
• 
To help students interact with industry professionals and plan career paths 
• 
To facilitate CSI certification and training programmes 
Objectives

• 
Lady Ada Programming Competition: Participation in ACM India-W's annual coding contest for female 
students 
Membership Details 
The ACM-W Chapter welcomes all students who support the mission of advancing women in computing. While focused on 
women's empowerment, membership is open to all genders who wish to contribute to creating an inclusive computing 
environment. Leadership and Faculty Coordinators 
Faculty Support: School of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence 
Collaborating Entities: GitHub Community SR University, Coding Club SR University 
Contact Information 
Institution: SR University, Warangal, Telangana 
Parent Organization: ACM-W India, Association for Computing Machinery 
Department: School of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence 
Achievements 
• 
Successful collaboration with GitHub Community for career development sessions 
• 
Active participation in ACM India-W programmes including Lady Ada Programming Competition 
• 
Regular technical and professional development workshops for women students

Student Life and Extracurricular Activities 
At SR University, we believe in the holistic development of our students. Alongside 
academics, we offer a variety of extracurricular activities, clubs, and organizations that 
cater to diverse interests, including sports, arts, cultural events, and community 
service. Club Management Portal 
The Club Management Portal is a dedicated platform designed to enhance student 
engagement and streamline the management of various clubs and organizations at SR 
University. This portal serves as a hub for students to discover, join, and participate in 
clubs that align with their interests. Key features of the portal include: 
• 
Club Discovery: Students can easily browse through a diverse range of clubs, 
each with detailed descriptions, activities, and upcoming events. • 
Event Management: Club leaders can organize events, manage registrations, 
and communicate with participants through the portal. • 
Member Management: Clubs can maintain an updated roster of members, 
track participation, and celebrate achievements.

• 
Collaboration Tools: The portal facilitates collaboration between different 
clubs, encouraging joint events and initiatives that promote teamwork and 
community spirit. By fostering active participation in clubs and activities, the Club Management Portal 
aims to create a vibrant student life that complements academic pursuits and 
prepares students for future challenges. Conclusion 
At SR University, we are dedicated to nurturing talent and fostering an environment 
of excellence. We invite you to join us on this exciting journey of learning, growth, 
and discovery.

Student Activities and Events
Student Clubs
SRU clubs help the student community to develop talents, interests and passion. Students 
are encouraged to participate in atleast one club every semester and expand their interests 
and skills. The main objective of various clubs is to identify the potential of every student and to 
facilitate them in achieving their academic goals through activities in these clubs. Open to 
all students, the clubs plan events and activities throughout the year. These clubs enrich 
the social, cultural and academic experience of students. It provides an exclusive platform 
for different dimension of learning, networking and socializing outside of the classroom. The following clubs are active for the academic year 2023-24:

Coding Club
In today’s rapidly changing environment, programming skills are 
essential tools that can be utilized and incorporated into various 
fields and domains. Hence, it becomes essential to equip young 
minds with such skills. Coding Club aims to establish a coding culture 
on campus, reaching every student passionate about coding. The 
club’s motto is to Create-Optimize-Debug-Execute. Data Science Club
Data science is a powerful tool that can be used to solve some 
of the world’s most pressing problems. We want to empower our 
students to use their skills to positively impact the world. We also 
strive to create a community where students can connect, share 
ideas, and collaborate on projects. Cyber Security Club
The Cyber Security Club is a student-run club with the goal of 
providing outside-of-class activities relevant to the industry. Attendees will leave with valuable experience proven to be useful 
during interviews and jobs. The club is open to everybody and no 
matter what experience level or major you are. Our goal is to expand 
our knowledge of cybersecurity and information security through 
hands-on experience and direct interaction with professionals in 
the field. AIML Club
Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Club helps to foster 
skills envisage the real world problems and attempts to upskill the 
talent and prepare for the Millennial break-through technologies 
which require a thorough understanding and grasp of various AI 
and ML driven devices ubiquitously
SANKHYA
SANKHYA is a club for students interested in mathematics and 
computing that offers a variety of extracurricular activities to further 
explore mathematical sciences. It aims to bring together individuals 
with a passion for mathematics, promote student collaboration, 
and expose them to educational and career opportunities. It hosts 
academic and social events all year long such as celebrations of 
National Mathematics Day and Pi-Day, Mathematics Olympiad etc.

Student Clubs @ Department of 
Electrical and Electronics Engineering (EEE)
Club Name
Description
ElectrAIfy Club
ElectrAIfy Club is an AI Club for Electrical Engineering. It is 
a dynamic and inclusive community of students passionate 
about AI, Machine Learning, and its applications in Electrical 
Engineering. This club aims to provide a platform for students 
to learn, collaborate, and innovate in the rapidly evolving field 
of AI applications in Electrical Engineering. Simulation Club
The Simulation Club is a dynamic and innovative organization which 
serves as a platform for students, enthusiasts, and professionals 
alike, who share a common passion for simulation technologies 
and their application in the field of electrical engineering. The club’s primary mission is to foster a vibrant community that 
promotes hands-on learning, creativity, and collaboration through 
simulation-based projects. By utilizing cutting-edge software and 
tools, the club aims to deepen participants’ understanding of 
complex electrical systems and enhance problem-solving skills. Electrical 
Engineering 
Association (EEA)
The Electrical Engineering Association (EEA) of SR University 
serves as a place of community for students to discuss, learn and 
develop technical and professional skills among the electrical 
engineering students. Members of the EEA are offered with a 
range of programs and activities such as seminars, workshops, 
technical quizzes, poster presentations, certifications, hackathons, 
project competitions and exposition. The EEA is supported by 
both internal and external experts who assist the students in 
strategizing and organizing activities that will help them enhance 
their knowledge and skills in Electrical Engineering. Student Clubs @ Department of 
Electronics & Communication Engineering (ECE)
Club Name
Description
Byte Optimizers Club
Byte Optimizers strives to encourage interest and knowledge in 
the Internet of Things (IoT). The club brings collectively students 
who are excited about emerging technologies, smart devices, and 
the potential of IoT to transform multiple sectors and daily life. Workshops, skill development, and invited talks will be among the 
activities offered by this club. Intelligence
Ignite Club
The Intelligence Ignite club amalgamates the most stimulating 
aspects of artificial intelligence (AI) and the cutting-edge field of 
Electronics and Communication Engineering (ECE). The club’s goal 
is to bridge the gap between AI and ECE by creating a collaborative 
environment in which students with diverse interests can investigate 
the synergy between these two fields. Intelligence Ignite’s activities 
will include guest lectures, workshops, and tutorials focusing on 
integrating AI techniques into ECE applications.

Socio Tech Innovative aims to encourage social innovation through 
the innovative use of technology and interdisciplinary approaches. By leveraging the power of technology and innovative thinking, 
the club hopes to address societal challenges and have a positive 
impact on local communities and beyond. This club’s activities 
include ideation and brainstorming sessions, workshops, and 
invited talks. Tech Connectors Club The Tech Connectors Club brings collectively students who are 
passionate about wireless communication and networking. The club 
focuses on the exciting world of wireless communication, including 
mobile networks, 5G, and beyond. It provides an opportunity for 
students to learn more about this rapidly changing field, exchange 
ideas, and gain practical knowledge of wireless communication 
systems. Wireless Technology Workshops, invited talks on wireless 
communication, and career opportunities are among the activities 
of this club. Student Clubs @ Department Mechanical Engineering (ME)

Marketing Club
The club’s main objective is to foster an environment 
that encourages creativity, innovation, and professional 
development in the field of marketing. This club will organize various activities such as workshops, 
industrial visits, competitions, student projects, and market 
research. Finance Club
The club aims to enhance students' understanding 
of the intricacies within the financial sector, fostering 
an environment that encourages study, contribution, 
participation, organization, and networking with industry 
professionals. Events organized by the club include 
workshops, seminars, and a guest speaker series, providing 
members with opportunities to explore financial topics and 
trends. Additionally, the club facilitates networking events, 
fostering connections between members and finance 
professionals, as well as encouraging collaboration among 
students passionate about finance. HR Club
The primary objective of the club is to shape students into 
future people managers, focusing on nurturing their potential 
for achieving excellence with a strong ethical foundation. The club envisions organizing various events, including 
team-building activities, problem-solving exercises, and 
other HR-related activities.
